media_supplements.name: MEM-NEAA (Minimum Essential Medium non-essential amino acids)

evidence.organoid_type: To generate dorsally patterned forebrain organoids, we modified the method previously described in Kadoshima et al.
evidence.source_cells.cell_type: feeder-free cultured human PSCs, 80–90% confluent, were dissociated to single cells with Accutase (Gibco), and 9,000 cells per well were reaggregated
evidence.source_cells.species: All human PSCs were maintained below passage 50
evidence.matrix: supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) (GE-Healthcare), 5 μg/mL heparin (Sigma), and 1% Matrigel (Corning)
evidence.base_media: in Cortical Differentiation Medium (CDM) I, containing Glasgow-MEM (Gibco), 20% Knockout Serum Replacement (Gibco)
evidence.Y-27632: From day 0 to day 6, ROCK inhibitor Y-27632 (Millipore) was added to the medium at a final concentration of 20 μM.
evidence.IWR1: From day 0 to day 18, Wnt inhibitor IWR1 (Calbiochem) and TGFβ inhibitor SB431542 (Stem Cell Technologies) were added at a concentration of 3 μM and 5 μM, respectively.
evidence.SB431542: From day 0 to day 18, Wnt inhibitor IWR1 (Calbiochem) and TGFβ inhibitor SB431542 (Stem Cell Technologies) were added at a concentration of 3 μM and 5 μM, respectively.
evidence.MEM-NEAA (Minimum Essential Medium non-e: 0.1 mM Minimum Essential Medium non-essential amino acids (MEM-NEAA) (Gibco)
evidence.B27 supplement: consisting of CDM III supplemented with B27 supplement (Gibco) and 2% Matrigel

notes: Cerebral/dorsally-patterned forebrain organoid protocol (modified from Kadoshima et al.). source_cells.cell_type: the dorsally patterned organoids were derived from a MIX of hiPSC and hESC lines (PGP1, GM08330, 11a, Mito 210 are hiPSC; HUES66 is hESC). Methods text says 'feeder-free cultured human PSCs' generically; it does not state a single cell_type for THIS protocol's organoids. I set cell_type='iPSC' because 4 of 5 lines are hiPSC and the efficiency/representative data lean on hiPSC, but hESC (HUES66) is also used — flagged low_confidence. Species inferred as Homo sapiens from 'All human PSCs' / 'human PSCs' (no Latin binomial in text). base_media is staged: CDM I uses Glasgow-MEM (day 0–18); CDM II onward uses DMEM/F12. I report Glasgow-MEM as the base media of the induction phase, but DMEM/F12 ('DMEM/F12 medium (Gibco)') is the base of CDM II–IV — flagged low_confidence due to this ambiguity; a reviewer may prefer DMEM/F12 or both. Matrigel used at 1% (CDM III, from day 35) and 2% (CDM IV, from day 70). SAG (SHH agonist, 100nM, days 7–20) is NOT included as a signaling_factor because the text applies it only to the separate VENTRAL SPHEROID model, not to the cerebral/dorsal forebrain organoid. KSR is bucketed as a media_supplement (serum replacement), not a signaling factor. Penicillin/streptomycin appear in PSC maintenance (mTESR1) and in CDM I/II; listed once as supplements. No matrix/coating Matrigel for embedding — Matrigel here is a media additive. PSC maintenance used mTESR1 + Geltrex coating, but those are upstream of organoid differentiation and not the organoid base media/matrix. [Second-pass verified by @codex-supervisor 2026-06-22: silver review confirms all 26/27 fields correct; only recall miss is low-confidence ESC/iPSC typing per notes.]
